new_classes.definition_en: A whisky produced by blending two or more single malt whiskies that have been distilled/obtained at different distilleries, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less (CN 2208 30 41) or more than 2 litres (CN 2208 30 49).

new_classes.label_en: blended malt Scotch whisky in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Malt Scotch Whisky in Behältern ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky produced by blending two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less.
A process in which two or more single malt whiskies, each from a different distillery, are combined to produce a blended malt whisky.
A quality borne by a blended malt whisky indicating that its constituent single malt whiskies originate from two or more different distilleries.
A quality borne by a whisky indicating that it qualifies as Scotch whisky under applicable regulations (i.e. distilled and matured in Scotland).

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) the product is a blended malt whisky produced from two or more single malt Scotch whiskies from different distilleries (modelled via BlendingProcess, MultiDistilleryOriginQuality, hasIngredient some SingleMaltWhisky, and complement of SingleDistilleryOriginQuality); (2) Scotch whisky origin (ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality); (3) the container size threshold of ≤ 2 L for CN 2208 30 41 (modelled via SmallContainer part restriction). A small gap remains because the legal note is shared with sibling code 22083049 (containers > 2 L), and the exact minimum number of contributing distilleries ('two or more') cannot be precisely expressed as an OWL cardinality constraint over named individuals without SWRL or additional machinery. The sibling class for CN 2208 30 49 (LargeContainer variant) is intentionally deferred to that code's axiom set.
